story: She’d been up since dawn, already out of ideas. Her seven-year-old missed friends; the pool yesterday and an expensive indoor playground hadn’t fixed it. He needed other boys, not a mom inventing games. At 9:42, the neighbor Mrs. Lee posted to the neighborhood chat: “Slip ’N Slide in our yard at 10 for kids 6–8—open invite.” The mother took a minute alone, breathing behind her locked bedroom door. The knob rattled;  She opened it. He stood there, new teeth too big for his face. “What are we doing today?” he asked.
implausible_knowledge: At 9:42, the neighbor Mrs. Lee posted to the neighborhood chat: “Slip ’N Slide in our yard at 10 for kids 6–8—open invite.”
question: When her son asks what they’re doing today, what will she most likely decide to do next?
answer_with_knowledge: Take him across the street to join the neighbors’ Slip ’N Slide with kids his age.
answer_without_knowledge: Suggest a simple at‑home plan, like setting up the sprinklers and a snack picnic in the yard.
